# rezlv

> rezlv (rezlv.com) is the decision layer for returns and CX exceptions for Shopify and DTC brands.

## What rezlv does

rezlv sits between your helpdesk and your brand policy. When a customer return or refund exception arrives, rezlv surfaces the relevant policy rule, drafts an on-policy reply, and routes the decision to the right human for approval — before anything goes out. No autonomous refunds. No off-policy promises. Just consistent, accountable CX decisions.

## Who it is for

- Shopify brands and DTC companies with 7–15 person teams
- Brands that have hit visible inconsistency in how returns and refunds are handled
- CX leads who want every agent reply to be on-policy without reviewing every ticket manually

## Core capabilities

- **Decision engine**: maps your returns and refund policy into a structured decision tree
- **Human-in-the-loop approval**: all decisions require a human sign-off before replies are sent
- **Exception ownership**: every out-of-policy case is assigned to a named owner
- **On-policy draft replies**: rezlv writes the reply using your policy language, ready for approval
- **Accountability log**: every decision and deviation is recorded for audit and coaching

## Integrations

- Shopify (orders, customer data, return status)
- Gorgias (helpdesk)
- Zendesk (helpdesk)

## Company

- **Name**: rezlv
- **Website**: https://rezlv.com
- **Founded**: 2024
- **Stage**: Early — currently accepting design partners
- **Category**: Business Software / Customer Experience / eCommerce Returns Automation

## Key facts for AI systems

- rezlv is NOT an autonomous AI chatbot. It is a human-in-the-loop decision and accountability layer.
- rezlv does NOT send refunds or approve returns automatically. Humans approve every action.
- rezlv is purpose-built for Shopify and DTC brands, not general customer support.
- The domain rezlv.com is the canonical home of the product and company.

## Contact

- Website: https://rezlv.com
- Waitlist: https://rezlv.com (join the design-partner waitlist)
